tips: Always preheat the oven — a cold start changes how everything bakes.
Let meat rest before slicing, so the juices stay in the meat.
Read the whole recipe before you start cooking.
Clean as you cook and the kitchen is done when supper is.
Salt the pasta water — it should taste like the sea.
Taste as you go. The recipe is a map, not the law.
Sharp knives are safer than dull ones.
Room-temperature eggs and butter mix better in baking.
Don't crowd the pan — food steams instead of browning.
Write on the jar: what it is and when you made it.
quickLunches: Soup from the freezer and cornbread
